surface.name: Semgrep Community Edition Python command-injection quickstart on Linux
surface.selection_basis: The official Community Edition quickstart is a self-contained no-account path. It fixes the sample file, registry ruleset, command, finding, and successful scan summary. Linux with pipx is selected from the documented operating-system installation matrix to remove installation ambiguity.

alternatives_considered.name: Semgrep AppSec Platform Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It requires GitHub or GitLab signup, organization creation, browser activation, and a repository, while the same official documentation offers Community Edition for a first local scan without an account.

alternatives_considered.name: macOS or Windows Community Edition installation
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are peer operating-system branches; Linux with pipx is explicitly selected as the reproducible candidate.

entry_point.developer_intent: Install Semgrep Community Edition on Linux and scan the official Python sample until Semgrep reports a successful scan and the documented command-injection finding.
assumed_prior_state: A Linux shell has Python 3.10 or later, pipx, network access to install Semgrep and retrieve registry rules, and permission to create a local file.
No Semgrep account, organization, repository integration, token, CLI installation, sample file, or prior scan is assumed.
No package installation, file creation, rule download, or scan was performed during research.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Semgrep Community Edition scans the official Python sample with the published Python command-injection ruleset and identifies the unsafe `os.system` call.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The terminal prints `Scan completed successfully.`, reports one blocking finding from 24 rules on one file, and points to `os.system("ls " + user_input)` under the documented command-injection rule.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Scan completed successfully. Findings: 1 (1 blocking). Ran 24 rules on 1 file: 1 finding.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Community Edition quickstart explicitly creates one vulnerable file, runs one scan, and shows the complete successful output before moving to rules, editors, CI, and additional learning.

prerequisites.requirement: Use Linux for the selected installation branch.
Have Python 3.10 or later and pipx installed.
Allow package installation and retrieval of the published rules from the Semgrep Registry.
Be able to create a text file and run shell commands in its directory.

primary_path.action: Open the Semgrep Community Edition quickstart and use its Linux instructions.
primary_path.output: Linux installation, sample creation, scan command, and expected output are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The page identifies the Community Edition first-scan flow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Another operating system requires a different documented installation branch.

primary_path.input: Python 3.10 or later, pipx, and package-network access.
primary_path.output: The `semgrep` executable is installed in the pipx environment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Python-version, package-index, network, or local-environment errors must be resolved before scanning.

primary_path.success_signal: A Semgrep version is displayed without a command-not-found error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the command is unavailable, fix the pipx path or installation before continuing.

primary_path.action: Create `app.py` with the exact vulnerable sample from the quickstart.
primary_path.output: A Python file containing the documented command-injection example exists.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different filename or sample changes the documented command and expected finding.

primary_path.success_signal: The scan command can address `app.py` by filename.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Running from another directory requires a different path.

primary_path.action: Run the published command-injection scan against the sample.
primary_path.input: The installed CLI, sample file, and network access to Semgrep Registry.
primary_path.output: Semgrep retrieves the ruleset and scans one target file.
primary_path.success_signal: The scan reaches its summary rather than stopping on configuration or target errors.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Network, registry, rule-configuration, or file-path errors can stop the scan before completion.

primary_path.action: Verify the successful scan summary and the command-injection finding.
details: Confirm one blocking finding, 24 rules, and one scanned target.
Confirm the finding points to the `os.system` line under the documented Python command-injection rule.
primary_path.output: The first Community Edition scan has succeeded and found the intended issue.
primary_path.success_signal: The terminal matches the guide's successful summary and finding location.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the output differs, verify the exact sample content, ruleset name, filename, and current CLI execution.

branches.path: Install with Homebrew, pipx, or uv as listed in the Community Edition quickstart, then converge on version verification and the same sample scan.
Follow the beta Windows Python instructions and UTF-8 guidance, then converge on the same sample scan.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds Windows-specific prerequisites and support limitations.

branches.path: Sign up with GitHub or GitLab, create an organization, choose Run on CLI, install Semgrep, run `semgrep login`, activate in the browser, run `semgrep ci`, and view findings in the platform.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the terminal from a local CLI finding to authenticated organization findings and adds account, permission, and browser steps.

branches.condition: Use another ruleset or local configuration
branches.path: Replace `p/python-command-injection` with another documented registry, automatic, or local config.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes rules run and expected findings, so it is outside the fixed quickstart candidate.

friction_gates.description: Installation commands vary by operating system and package manager; this candidate fixes Linux with pipx.
The Semgrep CLI requires Python 3.10 or later for this installation route.
The CLI must be installed before the first scan.
The deterministic quickstart result depends on creating the exact vulnerable Python sample.
The scan pulls the named rules from Semgrep Registry.

sources.title: Get started with Semgrep Community Edition - Semgrep
Quickstart - Semgrep
sections_used: Scan without GitHub or GitLab account

sources.title: Local scans with Semgrep - Semgrep
evidence_supported: `semgrep scan` recommendation for local no-account scanning

excluded_after_success: Fixing the finding, scanning a real repository, adding custom rules, signing in, organization policy, CI integration, editor integration, supply-chain scanning, deployment, and reporting occur after the first successful local scan.
